text:
In many artificial intelligence applications, the ability to generate well-calibrated probabilistic predictions is just as vital as achieving high classification accuracy. While many powerful machine learning models excel at discrimination, their raw outputs often fail to reflect true probabilities. To bridge this gap, we introduce Bayesian Binning into Quantiles (BBQ), a new non-parametric calibration framework designed to overcome the rigidities of traditional binning and scaling techniques.

Functioning as a flexible post-processing step, BBQ can be seamlessly integrated with any binary classification algorithm. Unlike standard histogram binning, which relies on a single fixed partition, our approach employs Bayesian model averaging to combine multiple binning schemes. This allows the model to remain robust against poorly chosen bin widths and irregular data distributions. Experimental evaluations across a diverse range of real-world and simulated datasets demonstrate that BBQ is computationally efficient and consistently out-performs common benchmarks—such as Platt scaling and isotonic regression—in producing accurate, reliable probability estimates.
